hallucinations.description: The paper reports identical performance metrics for the ERM and JTT methods down to four decimal places. Given that JTT involves a two-stage training process with upweighting of misclassified samples, it is practically impossible for it to yield the exact same average accuracy, worst-group accuracy, and average margin as standard ERM. Additionally, the paper claims to have run experiments across 3 random seeds, yet reports standard deviations of exactly 0.0000 for all metrics across all methods.
hallucinations.evidence:
Table 1: Overall Performance on Colored MNIST Test Set
| ERM | 0.9762 ± 0.0000 | 0.9717 ± 0.0000 | 8.7003 ± 0.0000 |
| JTT | 0.9762 ± 0.0000 | 0.9717 ± 0.0000 | 8.7003 ± 0.0000 |
... combined with the claim in Section 4.4: "Random seeds: 3 runs (seeds 42, 123, 456)"

hallucinations.description: The paper cites multiple nonexistent papers purportedly published in 2025 to support its literature review on annotation-free methods.
hallucinations.evidence: "Elastic Representation (ElRep) (Wen et al., 2025)" and "Data pruning approaches (Mulchandani & Kim, 2025)" in Section 2.3.

overall_assessment: The paper contains severe hallucinations, most notably in its experimental results. The reported metrics for different baselines are identical to four decimal places, and the standard deviations are zero despite claiming multiple seed runs, indicating that the data was fabricated rather than empirically derived. Furthermore, the paper includes fabricated citations from the future (2025).
